hypothesis: If DFT-refined force field parameters are combined with Bayesian optimization using polymer-specific molecular representations (PolyNC, polyBERT) and a GP with deep kernel learning plus Thompson sampling, then high-performance polyamines with greater solid-state CO2 adsorption capacity can be identified with significantly fewer simulations than when using generic representations or unrefined force fields, because the refined interaction parameters and tailored embeddings improve adsorption prediction accuracy and surrogate-model-driven search efficiency.
